channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Position Paper: Post-Solve Robustness in Decision Engines: Feasible Regions and Smoothness Under Perturbations
item.description: arXiv:2606.00002v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) decision engines routinely output nominally optimal plans for high-stakes industrial systems. Yet deployment rarely matches solve-time assumptions: small perturbations in costs, demands, or resource availability...

item.title: Emergent Collaborative Deliberation in Multi-Model AI Systems: A BFT-Derived Protocol for Epistemic Synthesis
item.description: arXiv:2606.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the Consilium Protocol, a Byzantine Fault Tolerance-derived architecture for structured multi-model AI deliberation that treats inter-model disagreement as epistemic signal rather than error. The protocol assigns engineered cognitive...

item.title: Deliberative Curation: A Protocol for Multi-Agent Knowledge Bases
item.description: arXiv:2606.00007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As AI agents transition from isolated tools to collaborative participants in shared knowledge ecosystems, governing collective knowledge curation becomes a critical challenge. Human platform governance mechanisms do not transfer directly: agent...

item.title: Agents on a Tree: Pathwise Coordination for Multi-Objective Molecular Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2606.00008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-objective molecular optimization requires searching vast chemical spaces under conflicting objectives, where early design decisions strongly constrain downstream outcomes. Existing methods typically rely on a single policy or fixed...

item.title: Optimal Transport-based Permutation-Invariant Bayesian Optimization of Offshore Wind Farm Layouts
item.description: arXiv:2606.00009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bayesian Optimization (BO) is widely and successfully adopted for solving optimization problems having an expensive-to-evaluate, black-box, and non-convex objective function. However, the vanilla BO algorithm is not able to exploit possible symmetries...

item.title: BitsMoE: Efficient Spectral Energy-Guided Bit Allocation for MoE LLM Quantization
item.description: arXiv:2606.00079v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) large language models reduce per-token computation through sparse expert activation, but their deployment remains memory-intensive because all expert weights must be kept resident in memory. Existing MoE compression methods...

item.title: DAStatFormer: A Hybrid Multibranch Transformer with Statistical Feature Integration for DAS-Based Pattern Recognitions
item.description: arXiv:2606.00081v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) enables large-scale monitoring through optical fibers, but its high dimensionality and complex spatio-temporal patterns make event classification demanding. Existing deep learning approaches-CNNs, recurrent models,...

item.title: Hoeffding Concept Bottleneck Models with Applications to Overhead Images
item.description: arXiv:2606.00082v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Explainability of deep learning algorithms is critical for computer-vision applications with high-stake decisions. Concept bottleneck models (CBM) have recently shown promising performance to provide explainable and accurate predictions for...

item.title: From Demonstrations to Rewards: Test-Time Prompt Optimization for VLM Reward Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.00083v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning relies on accurate reward functions, which are often hand-crafted or even unavailable in real-world applications, such as robotics. Recent work has explored the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of pre-trained Vision-Language...

item.title: A Shared Valence Axis Across Modern LLMs and Human EEG: The Saturation Regularity
item.description: arXiv:2606.00129v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have emerged as powerful representation learners whose internal features increasingly align with human cognition. We study whether modern LLMs can serve as a lens for understanding neural representations in the human...

item.title: DraDDP: A Multimodal Multi-Party Dialogue Discourse Parsing Dataset
item.description: arXiv:2606.00012v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-party dialogue discourse parsing aims to identify dependency structures and relation types between utterances in conversations. Previous studies are mostly limited to textual modality or two-party dialogue, failing to meet the multimodal and...

item.title: Toward Robust In-Context Learning: Leveraging Out-of-distribution Proxies for Target Inaccessible Demonstration Retrieval
item.description: arXiv:2606.00014v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although studies have demonstrated that Large Language Models (LLMs) can perform well on Out-of-Distribution (OOD) tasks, their advantage tends to diminish as the distribution shift becomes more severe. Consequently, researchers aim to retrieve...

item.title: AEyeDE: An Attention-Based Attribution Framework for AI-Generated Text Detection
item.description: arXiv:2606.00016v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Detecting AI-generated text is becoming increasingly challenging as modern language models approach human-level fluency and can evade detectors that rely on surface statistics or likelihood-based signals. We propose \textsc{AEyeDE}, an...

item.title: CSRP: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Chinese Text Correction via Reinforcement Learning with Efficiency-Aware Rewards
item.description: arXiv:2606.00020v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) based Chinese Grammatical Error Correction (CGEC) systems face two critical challenges: general-purpose models lack specialized linguistic priors for subtle grammatical distinctions, and Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with...

item.title: SENSE: Semantic Embedding Navigation with Soft-gated Evaluation for Retrieval-based Speculative Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2606.00021v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative Decoding (SD) accelerates Large Language Model (LLM) inference by employing a lightweight draft model to propose candidate tokens, which are verified in parallel by the target model, without compromising generation quality. While...

item.title: [AINews] NVIDIA Cosmos 3, Nemotron 3 Ultra, and RTX Spark
item.description: Jensen scores a huge win.

item.title: Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 5xx errors in the Miami and Bogota areas
item.description: Jun 2, 03:24 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 5xx errors between Miami and Bogota. We are working to analyse and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Maxio Technology Battles for Enterprise SSD Controller Market Dominance
item.description: Maxio Technology, China's leading SSD controller maker, navigates the high-stakes enterprise storage market where barriers to entry are steep but rewards are sustainable once established.

item.title: Tencent Games Unveils 42-Product Roadmap With AI Integration Strategy
item.description: Tencent Games showcased 42 products including 15 new titles at its annual conference, highlighting AI-powered game development and global expansion as key strategic priorities.

item.title: Alibaba's Qwen Team Enters Embodied AI With Qwen-VLA Model
item.description: Alibaba's Tongyi Qianwen team launched Qwen-VLA, its first vision-language-action model for embodied AI, signaling the company's entry into the physical world AI race.

item.title: Deep Principle Launches MPA, a 'Materials AlphaFold' Achieving SOTA on 40 Industrial Tasks
item.description: Deep Principle released MPA (Materials Property Axiom), an AI foundation model for materials science that achieves state-of-the-art results on 40 real-world industrial tasks by borrowing large language model training techniques.

item.title: Vbot Delivers First Robot Dog to Horizon Robotics Founder, Marking Consumer Embodied AI Milestone
item.description: Embodied AI company Vbot delivered its first robot dog to Horizon Robotics founder Dr. Yu Kai, becoming the first personal user of the quadruped robot and signaling the industry's transition from lab demos to consumer delivery.

item.title: Increased HTTP errors in Miami and Bogota
item.description: Jun 2, 01:30 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare saw an Increased HTTP errors in Miami and Bogota from 01:50 to 02:55 UTC. This issue is now resolved

item.title: Issue with TLS certificates using Lets Encrypt CA
item.description: Jun 2, 00:25 UTC Identified - Cloudflare has identified an issue affecting a subset of Let's Encrypt certificates, in which unsupported CA bundling may result in TLS connectivity issues for some visitors. Customers requiring immediate resolution may order a replacement certificate; re-issuance from...

item.title: Possible Network Congestion in US Eastern region
item.description: Jun 1, 22:38 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 21:34 UTC Update - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues. Jun 1, 21:31 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 21:05 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a...

item.title: Delays with Code Scanning and Billing
item.description: Jun 1, 19:27 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate delayed billing updates, on GitHub.com. We have applied additional mitigations are continuing to see signs of improvement, and are continuing to work to improve the processing rate. We will continue to keep users updated on progress towards...

item.title: Degraded performance for Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Jun 1, 19:06 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. Jun 1, 18:28 UTC Investigating - We are investigating elevated errors on requests to Claude Sonnet 4.6. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites
item.description: The security defect (CVE-2026-8732) allows unauthenticated attackers to create administrative accounts on the affected installations. The post WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet
item.description: Dutch authorities seized command-and-control servers tied to a botnet of infected computers, smartphones, and tablets that was allegedly used to power a residential proxy network and facilitate cybercrime. The post Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet appeared first on...

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: Jun 1, 17:48 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Cloudflare APIs that are specifically impacting OAuth/Wrangler API calls. These issues do not affect the serving of cached files via the Cloudflare CDN or other security features at the Cloudflare Edge. Customers using the...

item.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
item.description: A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm. "This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of...

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting...

item.title: Decreased ChatGPT availability for Free users
item.description: Status: ResolvedThe issue is resolved.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Introducing Mellum2: A 12B Mixture-of-Experts Model by JetBrains
Why Video Agent models are next — Ethan He, xAI Grok Imagine
item.description: Inside xAI: Building Grok Imagine in 3 Months, Videogen vs World Models, and why Grok Imagine is so underrated. For the first time, we do a deep dive with the guy who led it!

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Jun 1, 15:41 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 15:21 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 14:40 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs
item.description: Organizations are advised to patch CVE-2026-41089 as soon as possible, given its severity, the potential ongoing exploitation. The post Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
item.description: Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues. A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering...

item.title: Beyond LLMs: Why Scalable Enterprise AI Adoption Depends on Agent Logic
Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Jun 1, 13:33 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 12:48 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 12:19 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 1, 12:15 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Open and closed models are on different exponentials
item.description: Where marginally higher intelligence drives value, and where it doesn't.

item.title: Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus
item.description: Dragos said customers will soon gain expanded asset visibility and integrated device intelligence, with automated remediation workflows and a unified platform experience to follow. The post Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: China’s brain implant ambitions
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip—here’s what’s next Sitting in the courtyard of his house in China’s Henan province...

item.title: China-Aligned Groups Ramp Up Attacks: Dragon Weave Hits Czech Republic & Taiwan
item.description: A new cyber espionage campaign codenamed Operation Dragon Weave has been observed targeting officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan to deliver an AdaptixC2 agent. According to Seqrite Labs, targets of the campaign include government, research, academic, technology, and financial...

item.title: As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution
item.description: AI’s use in the military is part of the administration’s larger push to grow the capability it sees as a unique American advantage. The post As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
item.description: Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor. A...

item.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI. The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over...

item.title: Sonnet 4.5 elevated errors
item.description: Jun 1, 09:28 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 09:17 UTC Update - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues. Jun 1, 09:13 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 09:08 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating...

item.title: China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip—here’s what’s next
item.description: One day last October, sitting in the courtyard of his house in China’s Henan province, Dong Hui decided to see if he could hold a pen to write.  Dong, 39, had sustained spinal cord injuries in a car accident six years earlier that left him paralyzed from the neck down. Slowly but determinedly,...

item.title: Opus 4.7 elevated errors
item.description: Jun 1, 07:22 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 1, 07:08 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 1, 06:48 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.
